directions.name: Apply The Full PTX Prefetch Retime On The Accepted Export Base
directions.hypothesis: Round 15/17 closed the deeper seam shift: moving the pivot from 6144 to 5888 was worse, so the best seam member is already known. That leaves one last untested PTX family variant with a plausible upside: the full prefetch retime on top of the accepted export base, where both the initial two staging commits and the future refill use A-before-B ordering. Round 9 only tested the narrower future-refill retime. The full variant is the last meaningful scoreboard-oriented move still missing from the accepted export baseline.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Copy-pipeline handoff timing across both the initial stage prime and the future-tile refill in the PTX hot-band steady-state loop.
directions.risk: Medium. The family already had mixed results, and a full retime can again trade lower scoreboard for higher barrier, but this exact variant has not been tested on the accepted export base.
directions.stop_condition: Close the prefetch family if the full retime still fails to beat 24.84582424 ms or again just swaps scoreboard for barrier.

directions.name: Keep The 4-Row PTX Grouping As The Best Measured PTX Fallback
directions.hypothesis: If the full prefetch retime also stalls, the 4-row grouping remains the best measured PTX-adjacent fallback on top of the accepted export base. It still lost to the accepted base, but it outperformed the 2-row grouping, the broad controls, and the deeper seam shift.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual CTA grouping and orchestration overhead around the PTX hot-band grouped-row mapping.
directions.risk: Medium. The family already failed to beat the accepted base, so it should stay secondary behind the last untested prefetch variant.
directions.stop_condition: Only revisit if the full prefetch retime fails, and close it again immediately if it remains slower than the accepted base.

directions.name: Hold The 6144 Seam Variant As The Best Launch-Split Fallback
directions.hypothesis: The seam family already showed a local optimum at 6144 and then regressed at 5888. That makes the 6144 variant the best launch-split fallback if both the full prefetch retime and the 4-row grouping fail over the last rounds.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Boundary and launch split cost between the PTX hot-band kernel and the peeled 384-row row-band path.
directions.risk: Medium. The family has already produced its local best measured member, so it should stay tertiary rather than continue line-searching blindly.
directions.stop_condition: Only revisit this seam if the higher-ranked directions fail and avoid widening the change beyond the known 6144 local best.

notes: Round 16/17 diagnosis for run 20260420_163839_bf16_gemm_v1_fcc145b. Human-review mapping for this round: keep the broad 64x384 control and non-PTX 128x128 control closed, and now also stop line-searching deeper into the seam family because 5888 regressed relative to the 6144 pivot. The fresh evidence is that the seam family has a local best already, while the accepted export base still has one meaningful untested prefetch variant left: the full A-before-B retime across both prime and refill. No new explicit human idea family is queued in state/human_review.md, so the ranking for the penultimate round is: accept the full prefetch retime first, keep the 4-row grouping as the best measured PTX fallback, and retain the 6144 seam only as the best measured launch-split fallback.
